title: Treat the initial PRD as a rough intent draft
context: The first document is a brain dump for discussion, not a locked product direction.
decision: Use the PRD only as initial intent. Start by locking problem statement, scope, source eligibility, architecture, and execution model before implementation.
consequences: Avoid overfitting architecture to unvalidated draft details.
Expect docs and implementation plans to evolve as research lands.

title: Scope v1 around narrative screen media and exclude music
context: The motivating discovery problem is cross-domain recommendations and relationships for shows, movies, and anime. Music aggregation is a separate hard problem with mature external efforts.
decision: Make anime, TV, and movies the initial first-class domains. Exclude music from the initial scope.
consequences: Anime is not modeled as a TV subtype.
Music sources and schemas are deferred.
Book/source-material links may be used as relationship evidence without making books a full v1 domain.

title: Make anime first class
context: Anime has unique relationship, title, season/cour, OVA/ONA/special, and source-material realities. The manami project already proves substantial offline anime cross-reference work.
decision: Treat anime as a first-class domain alongside TV and movies, and leverage manami-project/anime-offline-database as a key source and design precedent.
consequences: Anime-specific schemas and relationship handling are expected.
Manami is a backbone candidate, not a thin afterthought wrapper.
Anime relationship richness can inform the shared relationship graph model.

title: Classify providers by admissible use
context: Provider research showed many popular APIs do not permit durable offline redistribution even for non-commercial open projects.
decision: Every source must be classified by role: BACKBONE_SOURCE, ID_SOURCE, LOCAL_EVIDENCE, RUNTIME_ONLY, or BLOCKED. Credentials unlock access but do not imply redistribution rights.
consequences: Provider adapters must enforce publishability at write time.
Restricted sources may help local matching but cannot leak raw payloads or copied metadata into public artifacts.
Source eligibility is central architecture, not a README footnote.

title: Use containerized execution as the local boundary
context: Long-running metadata jobs are risky if they only work on one developer machine. The project should match CI and scheduled execution from the start.
decision: Do not run project pipeline tasks directly on host Python. Local development uses Docker/Compose and Woodpecker CLI against the same command surface used by CI.
consequences: The Docker image is the canonical runtime.
Host Python packages are not validation dependencies.
Local bootstrap can use mounted caches and personal tokens, but it still runs inside containers.

title: Use Python with strict tooling inside the container
context: Python is not the preferred personal language, but it has the strongest data and Hugging Face ecosystem for this work.
decision: Use Python 3.12+, uv, Polars, PyArrow, DuckDB, Pydantic v2, httpx, Typer, huggingface_hub, sentence-transformers, pytest, Ruff, and Pyright inside the project container.
consequences: Keep Python code typed, linted, and production-shaped rather than notebook-shaped.
Do not rely on globally installed Python tools.
Use Pyright and Ruff as CI gates from the beginning.

title: Use polite cached batch LLMs for judgment, not generation
context: Free/open LLM capacity is available for open-source/non-commercial projects, but the project must not spam providers or generate unsupported facts.
decision: Use OpenAI-compatible LLM providers, initially OpenRouter, for bounded judgment tasks such as ambiguous match review, tag normalization, and relationship classification. Cache by input hash, prompt version, task, and model; publish decision logs.
consequences: LLM calls happen after deterministic and embedding filters.
Quota exhaustion should pause work and leave queued tasks rather than fail the whole pipeline.
LLM outputs are auditable derived judgments, not source metadata.
